title: Legislative Purpose
text: This Act is enacted to ensure the protection of trade secrets, maintain ethics of industrial competition, and ensure social and public interests. Where the matters are not provided for in this Act, the provisions of other relevant laws shall apply.

title: Definition of Trade Secret
text: The term 'trade secret' as used in this Act shall mean any method, technique, process, formula, program, design, or other information that may be used in the course of production, sales, or operations, and also meets the following requirements: 1. It is not known to persons generally involved in the information of this type; 2. It has economic value, actual or potential, due to its secretive nature; and 3. Its owner has taken reasonable measures to maintain its secrecy.

title: Ownership of Trade Secret Derived from Employment
text: Where a trade secret is developed by an employee in the course of performing his/her duties, such trade secret shall belong to his/her employer, unless otherwise agreed in the employment contract.

title: Acts Constituting Misappropriation
text: Any of the following acts shall be deemed as misappropriation of a trade secret: 1. Acquiring a trade secret by improper means; 2. Acquiring, using, or disclosing a trade secret with the knowledge or with reason to know that it was acquired by improper means; 3. Using or disclosing a trade secret acquired legitimately but in breach of a duty to maintain confidentiality.

title: Right to Injunction and Damages
text: Where a trade secret has been misappropriated, the owner of the trade secret may request the court to issue an injunction to prevent the act of misappropriation, and may also claim for damages.

title: Calculation of Damages
text: When claiming damages under Article 11, the owner of the trade secret may elect one of the following methods of calculation: 1. Damages provided for in Article 216 of the Civil Code; 2. The profits gained by the infringer through the act of infringement; 3. A reasonable royalty for the licensed use of the trade secret. Where the act of infringement was committed intentionally, the court may, upon request, award damages in an amount up to three times the proven amount.

title: Criminal Liability
text: Any person who commits any of the following acts with the intent to derive unlawful benefits for himself or for any third person, or to inflict damage on the owner of a trade secret, shall be subject to a sentence of imprisonment up to 5 years, and may, in addition, be fined NT$1,000,000 to NT$10,000,000.
